CountEverest Examples

CountEverest is a lightweight, customizable countdown script that's easy to integrate into your projects. It offers a range of features including callback functions, left-hand zeros, and easy localization. Perfect for developers who want a flexible countdown solution without the need for extensive JavaScript knowledge.

Explore the various examples below to see CountEverest in action. For more information and to access the source code, visit the CountEverest GitHub repository.

Auto-Initialization (New in 3.1.0)

Countdown timers that start automatically when scrolled into view - no JavaScript required!

View Example

Callback Feature

Execute custom code when countdowns complete - show messages, trigger animations, or run any code.

View Example

No theme: Plain Text Countdown

Just that 1.4KB of sweet vanilla JS.

View Example

Theme 1: Grid Countdown

A simple and elegant countdown display with days, hours, minutes, and seconds.

View Example

Theme 6: Colorful Blocks

Countdown displayed in colorful blocks for each time unit.

View Example

Theme 9: Flip Clock Style

Countdown that mimics a flip clock animation.

View Example

Theme 10: Minimal Circles

A clean, minimalist design with circular elements for each time unit.

View Example

Theme 12: Video Background

Countdown with a video background for a dynamic and engaging effect.

View Example
